title: Quiz: staying reliable when a third-party API is flaky
prompt_md: Your API depends on a third-party service that is sometimes slow and sometimes fails. How do you keep your own service reliable?
options: Cache the third-party's previous response forever so you never have to call it again
Call it normally and let the request hang until the third-party responds, however long it takes
Set a timeout on the call, retry transient failures with exponential backoff and jitter, and add a circuit breaker to fail fast when it's down — optionally offloading the work to a queue
Retry the call immediately in a tight loop until it eventually succeeds
explanation_md: Layer the standard **resilience patterns**. A **timeout** stops a hung dependency from tying up your threads/connections. **Retries with exponential backoff and jitter** handle transient blips without synchronizing into a **thundering herd**. A **circuit breaker** trips open after repeated failures so you **fail fast** during a cooldown instead of cascading the outage into your service. For non-urgent work, push it onto a **queue/background job** so the request returns immediately and retries happen out-of-band. An immediate tight-loop retry just amplifies the outage; an infinite cache serves dangerously stale data.
